summary.text: The dominant pattern today is the sustained strength of AI Agent frameworks, led by nesquena/hermes-webui (+1,815 stars in 24h) and supermemoryai/supermemory (+801 stars), both maintaining hot momentum from prior days. MCP/Tool Calling category also remains active with D4Vinci/Scrapling (+1,656 stars) as a top gainer. A notable acceleration anomaly appears in Distributed Training and Local LLM categories: DeepSpeed (3.5x weekly acceleration) and ncnn (2.5x) broke out from low baseline activity, suggesting renewed developer interest in infrastructure optimization. Cross-source confirmation is weak—only Anthropic's S-1 filing appears on both GitHub (via news) and Hacker News, but no product launches align with GitHub trends. The absence of Coding Agent / SWE Agent projects from the top movers despite high weekly averages may indicate a cooling-off period after recent spikes.

projects.name: hermes-webui
projects.description: Hermes WebUI: The best way to use Hermes Agent from the web or from your phone!

llmSummary.oneLiner: A lightweight, dark-themed web UI for Hermes Agent that mirrors the CLI experience with persistent memory, scheduling, and multi-platform access.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Most AI tools reset every session, requiring users to re-explain context. Hermes WebUI provides a self-hosted, persistent agent with cross-session memory, scheduled jobs, and a full-featured web interface accessible from any device.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo gained 1,815 stars in the last 24 hours, reaching 12,002 total stars with 1,501 forks. Despite a low acceleration ratio (1.0), the sustained high velocity and recent release within 30 days indicate strong ongoing interest. The project is clearly resonating with developers seeking self-hosted, persistent AI agents.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. The recent release and active commits suggest continuous improvement, and the README includes a detailed comparison table against competitors like OpenClaw and Claude Code, which may be driving awareness.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Hermes WebUI addresses a growing demand for self-hosted, always-on AI agents that remember context across sessions. Its feature parity with CLI, support for multiple providers, and integration with messaging platforms make it a compelling alternative to cloud-dependent tools.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: For developers building agentic workflows, Hermes WebUI offers a ready-to-deploy solution with persistent memory, scheduling, and a clean web interface. It's particularly useful for teams that need privacy, customization, and offline capabilities.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward self-hosted, memory-persistent AI agents as a practical alternative to stateless chat interfaces. Developers should consider how persistent context and scheduled automation can differentiate their own AI products.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers and power users who want a self-hosted AI agent with persistent memory, scheduling, and multi-platform access, especially those already using Hermes Agent or seeking an open-source alternative to Claude Code or OpenClaw.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is tied to Hermes Agent, which may have its own adoption risks. Native Windows support is limited, and the community notes some POSIX path issues. The rapid star growth could attract maintenance challenges.

projects.name: Scrapling
projects.description: 🕷️ An adaptive Web Scraping framework that handles everything from a single request to a full-scale crawl!

llmSummary.oneLiner: An adaptive web scraping framework that auto-relocates elements when sites change, bypasses anti-bot systems, and scales from single requests to full crawls.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Web scrapers break when websites update their HTML structure, and anti-bot measures like Cloudflare Turnstile block traditional tools. Scrapling's parser learns from changes and automatically finds elements again, while its fetchers handle anti-bot bypass out of the box.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 57,984 stars and a 24-hour delta of 1,809, the project is experiencing sustained, high-velocity growth. The acceleration ratio is low (1), indicating steady organic interest rather than a sudden spike. This consistent attention suggests the project has become a go-to tool in the web scraping community.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent commits and a release within 30 days show active maintenance, but no external buzz or specific event is identifiable.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It combines adaptive parsing (auto-relocation of elements), anti-bot bypass, and a full spider framework in one library, addressing pain points that existing tools handle separately. The MCP server integration also taps into the AI agent ecosystem.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Scrapling reduces maintenance overhead for scrapers by automatically adapting to website changes, and its built-in anti-bot bypass simplifies data extraction from protected sites.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, Scrapling's MCP server and agent skill directory signal a shift toward making web scraping a first-class capability for AI agents, enabling autonomous data collection without constant manual selector updates.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Python developers building web scrapers, data extraction pipelines, or AI agents that need to collect data from modern websites with anti-bot protections.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new (first release likely in 2025) and may have undiscovered edge cases. Reliance on anti-bot bypass techniques could be fragile if target sites update their defenses. The README includes sponsored proxy services, which may introduce bias in recommendations.

projects.name: supermemory
projects.description: Memory engine and app that is extremely fast, scalable. The Memory API for the AI era.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Supermemory is a state-of-the-art memory and context engine for AI, providing persistent memory, user profiles, hybrid search, and connectors via a single API.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI assistants forget everything between conversations. Supermemory automatically extracts facts, builds user profiles, handles knowledge updates and contradictions, forgets expired information, and delivers the right context at the right time.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 23,845 stars, a 24-hour star delta of 752, and a sustained hot trend, Supermemory is experiencing strong, consistent growth. The project has recent commits and a release within 30 days, indicating active development. Its #1 ranking on three major AI memory benchmarks (LongMemEval, LoCoMo, ConvoMem) and the release of plugins for Claude Code, OpenCode, and others are driving attention.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. Recent activity includes new plugins for AI coding tools and continued benchmark leadership.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Supermemory solves a fundamental pain point for AI users and developers: persistent memory. It offers a complete, easy-to-integrate solution with high benchmark performance, attracting both end-users (via app and MCP) and developers (via API and framework integrations).
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Supermemory provides a drop-in memory layer for any AI app or agent, eliminating the need to build custom vector DB pipelines, embedding strategies, or chunking logic. It supports major frameworks (Vercel AI SDK, LangChain, Mastra, etc.) and offers a simple API for storing, searching, and profiling user context.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward commoditized memory infrastructure for AI. Developers no longer need to build custom memory solutions; they can rely on a specialized, benchmark-optimized service. The rise of MCP and plugin integrations suggests memory will become a standard layer in AI toolchains.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers building agents or apps that need persistent user memory, context, and RAG; also end-users who want their AI assistants to remember preferences and past conversations.
llmSummary.riskNotes: As a hosted service, there are potential concerns around data privacy, vendor lock-in, and latency. The project is open-source but the core memory engine may be proprietary. Competition from other memory solutions (Mem0, Zep) could fragment the market.

projects.name: DeepSpeed
projects.description: DeepSpeed is a deep learning optimization library that makes distributed training and inference easy, efficient, and effective.

llmSummary.oneLiner: DeepSpeed is a deep learning optimization library for distributed training and inference, enabling efficient scaling to trillion-parameter models.
llmSummary.problemSolved: DeepSpeed solves the challenge of training and deploying large-scale deep learning models (billions to trillions of parameters) by providing memory and compute optimizations like ZeRO, pipeline parallelism, and mixed precision, making distributed training accessible and efficient on limited hardware.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 42,440 stars, a 24-hour delta of 22, and a 7-day delta of 46, DeepSpeed shows consistent high engagement. The acceleration ratio of 3.5 (high) indicates a sudden breakout in attention, likely driven by recent releases (e.g., Muon Optimizer support, SDMA for ZeRO-3) and community events (ASPLOS 2026 tutorial, meetups). The project's sustained velocity and recent spikes make it a top-tier optimization library for AI practitioners.
llmSummary.whatChanged: Recent activity includes a May 2026 blog on Muon Optimizer support, a May 2026 feature for System DMA (SDMA) on AMD GPUs, and an ASPLOS 2026 tutorial. These updates, along with a recent release within 30 days and active commits, signal ongoing innovation and community engagement.
llmSummary.whyTrending: DeepSpeed is trending due to its critical role in enabling large-scale AI training, continuous addition of cutting-edge features (e.g., Muon optimizer, SDMA, SuperOffload), and strong ecosystem integrations with Hugging Face, PyTorch Lightning, and others. The project's high star count and recent acceleration reflect its importance in the AI infrastructure landscape.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: DeepSpeed is essential for any developer working with large models (1B+ parameters) who needs to optimize training or inference on limited GPU resources. Its ZeRO family and parallelism techniques can reduce memory footprint and speed up training, making it a must-try for scaling experiments.
llmSummary.developerInsight: DeepSpeed's trajectory signals a shift toward more efficient, hardware-aware distributed training. Developers should adopt it to stay competitive in training large models, especially with new features like Muon optimizer and SDMA that push performance boundaries on diverse hardware (NVIDIA, AMD, Intel).
llmSummary.targetUsers: Machine learning engineers, researchers, and data scientists training or deploying large-scale deep learning models (e.g., LLMs, MoE) who need to optimize memory, speed, and scalability across multi-GPU or multi-node setups.
llmSummary.riskNotes: DeepSpeed is complex to configure for beginners, and its tight coupling with PyTorch may limit flexibility. Some features (e.g., specific ops) require CUDA or custom hardware support, and the project's rapid evolution could introduce breaking changes. Additionally, the high number of open issues (1,291) suggests potential maintenance challenges.

projects.name: ncnn
projects.description: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for the mobile platform

llmSummary.oneLiner: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for mobile, embedded, and desktop deployment, with CPU and Vulkan GPU backends.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Deploying deep learning models efficiently on resource-constrained devices (phones, embedded systems, browsers) without heavy dependencies.
llmSummary.whyNow: The project shows a sudden breakout with a 2.3x acceleration ratio and a 7-star delta in the last 24 hours, despite a slight dip yesterday. With 23,318 stars and a recent release, it's gaining renewed attention as mobile and edge AI deployment becomes critical.
llmSummary.whatChanged: A recent release (within 30 days) and active commits within the last 7 days suggest ongoing improvements. The README highlights new platform support (HarmonyOS, visionOS) and the pnnx tool for PyTorch/ONNX conversion, which likely drives interest.
llmSummary.whyTrending: ncnn is a mature, battle-tested framework from Tencent, used in major apps like WeChat and QQ. Its zero-dependency design and broad platform support make it a go-to for on-device AI, especially as edge inference gains momentum.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: If you need to run neural networks on mobile or embedded devices without heavy frameworks, ncnn offers a lightweight, high-performance solution with easy PyTorch/ONNX conversion via pnnx.
llmSummary.developerInsight: For AI developers, ncnn signals a shift toward efficient on-device inference, reducing reliance on cloud APIs. It's particularly valuable for privacy-sensitive apps and real-time applications.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Mobile developers, embedded systems engineers, AI researchers deploying models on edge devices, and anyone needing fast inference on Android, iOS, or Linux.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project has 1,175 open issues, indicating active community support but also potential bugs or feature requests. Vulkan GPU support may not be available on all devices.

projects.name: cognee
projects.description: Memory platform for AI Agents in 6 lines of code

llmSummary.oneLiner: Cognee is an open-source memory control plane for AI agents that ingests data, builds knowledge graphs, and provides persistent, context-aware recall with just a few lines of code.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI agents lack persistent, evolving memory across sessions and data sources, leading to repetitive mistakes and loss of context. Cognee solves this by unifying ingestion, graph/vector search, and feedback loops into a single memory layer.
llmSummary.whyNow: Cognee is experiencing a sudden breakout with 27 stars in the last 24 hours and a 7-day average of 16 stars per day, totaling 17,636 stars. The acceleration ratio of 2.3 indicates a sharp increase in attention, likely driven by recent releases (within 30 days) and active development (commits within 7 days). The project's focus on persistent memory for agents aligns with the growing demand for production-ready AI agent infrastructure.
llmSummary.whatChanged: No single obvious trigger — the project appears to be sustaining organic growth. However, the recent release within 30 days and active commits suggest ongoing feature improvements and bug fixes that may have attracted new users.
llmSummary.whyTrending: Cognee addresses a critical pain point for AI developers: giving agents long-term memory. Its simple API (remember, recall, forget, improve), support for multiple LLM providers, and integrations with Claude Code and OpenClaw make it easy to adopt. The project's active community, clear documentation, and deployment options (cloud, self-hosted) further fuel its popularity.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Cognee offers a straightforward way to add persistent memory to AI agents, reducing the need to build custom memory systems. Its graph-based approach and feedback loops enable agents to learn and improve over time, making it a valuable tool for production deployments.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward memory-first agent architectures. Developers should consider integrating persistent memory layers to improve agent reliability and context retention, especially for customer support, knowledge management, and copilot use cases.
llmSummary.targetUsers: AI developers building agents for customer support, knowledge management, copilots, and any application requiring long-term context and learning from interactions.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new and may have limited production maturity. Dependency on specific LLM providers (e.g., OpenAI) could be a risk for some users. The rapid growth may strain community support and documentation.

projects.name: NextChat
projects.description: ✨ Light and Fast AI Assistant. Support: Web | iOS | MacOS | Android |  Linux | Windows

llmSummary.oneLiner: NextChat is a lightweight, cross-platform AI chat client that supports multiple LLMs (Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, DeepSeek) with one-click deployment and a privacy-first local-storage design.
llmSummary.problemSolved: It provides a unified, fast, and privacy-respecting interface for interacting with various AI models, eliminating the need to switch between different chat UIs or expose API keys to third-party services.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 88,131 stars and a 24h star delta of 22, the project shows sustained high interest. The acceleration ratio of 2.9 (high) indicates a sudden breakout in attention, likely driven by recent feature additions like MCP support, real-time chat, and the iOS app release. The 7-day average star delta of 7.8 confirms consistent growth, but the acceleration signals a recent spike.
llmSummary.whatChanged: Recent commits and README updates highlight the addition of MCP (Model Context Protocol) support, real-time chat, and the launch of the iOS app. These features expand the project's utility and appeal, likely triggering the breakout.
llmSummary.whyTrending: NextChat combines ease of deployment (one-click Vercel), broad model support, and a polished cross-platform desktop app, making it a go-to tool for developers and power users who want a self-hosted, customizable AI assistant.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: This project demonstrates the demand for lightweight, multi-model chat UIs that prioritize privacy and local storage. Developers can learn from its architecture (Next.js + Tauri) and plugin system to build similar tools.
llmSummary.developerInsight: The rapid adoption of NextChat signals a shift toward unified, self-hosted AI interfaces that reduce dependency on single providers. The MCP support hints at a future where AI tools interoperate via standardized protocols, opening opportunities for plugin ecosystems.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers, AI enthusiasts, and power users who want a fast, private, and customizable chat interface for multiple LLMs, including those running local models.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project relies on third-party API keys and services; users must manage their own keys. The rapid pace of features may introduce instability. No recent release tag could indicate a focus on bleeding-edge commits over stable releases.

projects.name: DeepSeek-Reasonix
projects.description: DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for your terminal. Engineered around prefix-cache stability — leave it running.

llmSummary.oneLiner: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for prefix-cache stability and low token costs.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Reduces token costs and latency in long AI coding sessions by leveraging DeepSeek's prefix cache, while providing a configurable, plugin-driven agent that runs as a single static binary.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 16,367 stars and a 7-day average delta of 1,210.9 stars per day, this project is experiencing sustained explosive growth. The 24-hour delta of 923 stars and acceleration ratio of 1.1 indicate continued high interest without sudden spikes, suggesting organic adoption. It ranks top 2 in Agents velocity on oosmetrics, signaling strong community validation.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The 1.0 ground-up rewrite in Go (main-v2 branch) is the likely catalyst, offering a single binary, improved performance, and MCP plugin support. The README highlights this migration, and the project has a recent release within 30 days, with active commits.
llmSummary.whyTrending: DeepSeek's rising popularity, combined with the agent's focus on cost efficiency via prefix caching and a clean config-driven design, appeals to developers seeking a lightweight, terminal-based AI coding assistant. The Go rewrite and MCP compatibility broaden its appeal.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: A practical, low-cost AI coding agent optimized for DeepSeek, with a plugin system and sandboxed permissions, ready for daily terminal use.
llmSummary.developerInsight: Signals a shift toward specialized, model-native agents that optimize for API economics (prefix caching) and developer ergonomics (single binary, config-driven). The MCP plugin support positions it as a hub for tool integration.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using DeepSeek models who want a terminal-based AI coding agent with low token costs, configurable tools, and plugin extensibility.
llmSummary.riskNotes: Heavy reliance on DeepSeek's API and prefix cache; if DeepSeek changes pricing or cache behavior, the value proposition weakens. The project is in early Go rewrite (1.0 not yet on npm), so stability may vary. 465 open issues suggest active development but also potential rough edges.

projects.name: code-review-graph
projects.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI that builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, achieving 38x to 528x token reduction on reviews.
llmSummary.problemSolved: AI coding tools waste tokens by re-reading large parts of the codebase on review tasks. code-review-graph builds a structural map using Tree-sitter, tracks changes incrementally, and provides precise context via MCP, reducing token consumption dramatically.
llmSummary.whyNow: The repo has 17,511 stars, a 24h star delta of 107, a three-day average of 70, and an acceleration of 1.5 (medium). It has a recent release within 30 days and a commit within 7 days. These metrics indicate sustained high interest and active development, making it a hot project for AI-assisted coding workflows.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The trigger is unclear from the provided data, but the sustained star growth and recent commits suggest ongoing improvements, possibly new platform integrations or performance enhancements.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It solves a critical pain point for developers using AI coding assistants: token waste and context overload. The dramatic token reductions (38x to 528x) and support for many platforms (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) make it highly appealing.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Integrating code-review-graph into your AI coding workflow can drastically reduce token usage and improve review accuracy. It is easy to install and supports incremental updates, making it practical for large monorepos.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward local-first, graph-based context management for AI coding tools. Developers should consider adopting such tools to reduce costs and improve AI response quality, and tool builders may want to integrate similar graph-based context retrieval.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, etc.) who work on large codebases or monorepos and want to reduce token consumption and improve review accuracy.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is relatively new and may have limitations in search quality (MRR 0.35) and flow detection (33% recall). It requires Python 3.10+ and may not support all languages equally. The 100 open issues indicate active development but also potential bugs.

projects.name: open-design
projects.description: 🎨 Local-first, open-source Claude Design alternative. 🖥️ Native desktop app. ⚡ 259+ Skills · ✨ 142+ Design Systems 🖼️ Web · desktop · mobile prototypes · slides · images · videos · HyperFrames 📦 Sandboxed preview · HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4 export 🤖 Claude Code / OpenClaw / Codex / Cursor / OpenCode / Qwen / Copilot / Hermes / Kimi & 17+ CLIs.

llmSummary.oneLiner: Open-source, local-first alternative to Claude Design that wires 16 coding-agent CLIs into a skill-driven design workflow for web, desktop, and mobile prototypes.
llmSummary.problemSolved: Claude Design is closed-source, paid-only, cloud-only, and locked to Anthropic's model. Open Design provides a fully open, local-first, BYOK alternative that lets developers use any coding agent CLI to generate design artifacts without vendor lock-in.
llmSummary.whyNow: With 57,300 stars, a 24-hour delta of +718, a 7-day delta of +5,008, and an acceleration of 1.3, this project is sustaining explosive growth. The 0.8.0-preview release and the README's call to action signal a major push to solidify the open-source alternative to Claude Design. The community is actively contributing, with 334 open issues and frequent commits.
llmSummary.whatChanged: The 0.8.0-preview release is the most recent trigger, accompanied by a README overhaul that emphasizes the project's mission and invites community participation. The project has been iterating fast on main, with recent commits and a release within 30 days.
llmSummary.whyTrending: It fills a massive gap: a free, open-source, local-first design tool that leverages existing coding agents. The viral success of Claude Design created demand for an open alternative, and Open Design delivers with 16 CLI integrations, 139 skills, and 150 design systems. The project's rapid star growth reflects this pent-up demand.
llmSummary.developerTakeaway: Open Design demonstrates that composable, agent-driven workflows can replace monolithic design tools. Developers can integrate their preferred coding agent CLI and customize skills and design systems, making it a flexible platform for AI-assisted design.
llmSummary.developerInsight: This signals a shift toward modular, agent-agnostic design tools. The BYOK approach and local-first architecture reduce dependency on any single AI provider, which is a key trend for enterprise adoption. Developers should watch for similar patterns in other creative tools.
llmSummary.targetUsers: Developers, designers, and product builders who want an open-source, local-first design tool that integrates with their existing coding agent CLI. Also suitable for teams seeking to avoid vendor lock-in with Claude Design or Figma.
llmSummary.riskNotes: The project is in preview (0.8.0) and may have stability issues. It relies on external CLI tools that may change or break. The rapid growth could lead to maintenance challenges. The README's aggressive tone may alienate some users.

modelDemoSignals.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
modelDemoSignals.description: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response
modelDemoSignals.summary: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response text instead of their own visual perception, leading to inconsistent and non-verifiable evaluations. To address this issue, we introduce the Perceptually Perturbed Judgment Dataset, which constructs minimally edited counterfactual responses that isolate perceptual errors and enable verifiable supervision. Building on this dataset, we develop a unified training framework that combines a structured GRPO-based reward with a batch-ranking objective, achieving coherent global ordering without explicit pairwise labels. Experiments across diverse MLLM-as-a-Judge benchmarks show that our approach substantially improves perceptual fidelity, ranking coherence, and alignment with human evaluation. Our results establish a scalable and generalizable pathway for training multimodal judges that are perceptually grounded, interpretable, and robust to visual-reasoning conflicts.
modelDemoSignals.author: Seojeong Park, Jiho Choi, Junyong Kang, Seonho Lee, Jaeyo Shin, Hyunjung Shim
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, alignment, benchmark, evaluation

modelDemoSignals.title: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
modelDemoSignals.description: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong performance through instruction tuning, but real-world deployment requires them to continually acquire new vision-language capabilities, making Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) essential. To reduce inter-task interference and promote collaboration, recent methods often employ sparse architectures like Mixture of LoRA Experts with image-text similarity routing. However, tasks with distinct response structures could share highly
modelDemoSignals.summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong performance through instruction tuning, but real-world deployment requires them to continually acquire new vision-language capabilities, making Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) essential. To reduce inter-task interference and promote collaboration, recent methods often employ sparse architectures like Mixture of LoRA Experts with image-text similarity routing. However, tasks with distinct response structures could share highly similar visual-linguistic semantics and thus be wrongly routed to the same expert; image-text similarity alone is insufficient for reliable task assignment. For example, an expert in a grounding task requiring coordinate prediction may be biased toward producing short textual answers after learning semantically similar VQA tasks. This format-blind task assignment integrates heterogeneous response types into shared parameters, inducing gradient interference and ineffective expert collaboration. To address this problem, we propose ProtoAda, a prototype-guided adaptive tuning framework. ProtoAda introduces format-aware task prototypes to align task assignment and routing with both task semantics and output structure, and further consolidates format-compatible updates in a geometry-aware manner to effectively reuse and progressively refine existing parameters. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that ProtoAda achieves superior performance, especially on tasks whose answer structures are easily corrupted by sequential tuning.
modelDemoSignals.author: Yu-Cheng Shi, Zhen-Hao Xie, Jun-Tao Tang, Da-Wei Zhou
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, benchmark

modelDemoSignals.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
modelDemoSignals.description: Video is temporally redundant: adjacent frames usually share most objects, background, and layout. Yet existing video multimodal large language models (video MLLMs) usually encode each sampled frame as an independent RGB image, causing visual tokens to repeat content already present in earlier frames. This suggests a more direct video interface: send a full reference frame only when the scene cannot be predicted well from prior context, and otherwise transmit a compact description of inter-frame
modelDemoSignals.summary: Video is temporally redundant: adjacent frames usually share most objects, background, and layout. Yet existing video multimodal large language models (video MLLMs) usually encode each sampled frame as an independent RGB image, causing visual tokens to repeat content already present in earlier frames. This suggests a more direct video interface: send a full reference frame only when the scene cannot be predicted well from prior context, and otherwise transmit a compact description of inter-frame changes. We call this interface a \emph{predictive visual code}, and instantiate it for video MLLMs as \textbf{AdaCodec}. AdaCodec spends full visual tokens on a reference frame only when its conditional predictive cost is high; otherwise, it encodes inter-frame changes, including motion and prediction residuals, as compact P-tokens. Across all eleven benchmarks, AdaCodec improves over the Qwen3-VL-8B per-frame RGB baseline at a matched visual-token budget. Even at $1/7$ the budget, AdaCodec with 32k tokens surpasses the 224k baseline on all long-video benchmarks; on five general-video benchmarks, it raises the average score while substantially cutting time-to-first-token from 9.26s to 1.62s.
modelDemoSignals.author: Haowen Hou, Zhen Huang, Zheming Liang, Qingyi Si, Chenglin Li, Shuai Dong, Kele Shao, Ruilin Li, Dianyi Wang, Nan Duan, Jiaqi Wang
modelDemoSignals.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, rag, benchmark

modelDemoSignals.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
modelDemoSignals.description: Clinical practice is not the selection of an answer from enumerated options: a physician gathers heterogeneous information incrementally and commits to sequential, irreversible decisions under uncertainty. Static benchmarks cannot probe and existing interactive medical benchmarks each compromise on at least one of them. We present ClinEnv, an interactive benchmark that evaluates LLMs as attending physicians over real inpatient admissions under a paradigm we term Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation
modelDemoSignals.summary: Clinical practice is not the selection of an answer from enumerated options: a physician gathers heterogeneous information incrementally and commits to sequential, irreversible decisions under uncertainty. Static benchmarks cannot probe and existing interactive medical benchmarks each compromise on at least one of them. We present ClinEnv, an interactive benchmark that evaluates LLMs as attending physicians over real inpatient admissions under a paradigm we term Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation. Each case is automatically constructed into an ordered sequence of decision stages; at every stage the model must actively query four specialized agents before committing to medications, procedures, and diagnoses. ClinEnv scores both what the model decides, through deterministic ontology-grounded matching, and how it gathers information. Across seven models, the strongest reaches only 0.31 decision F1, and outcome quality is sharply decoupled from process quality. Difficulty concentrates in management decisions and later stages, where models recover discharge diagnoses far more reliably than management actions (0.51 vs. 0.17 F1) and continue to issue redundant queries as cases progress. ClinEnv makes this information-acquisition gap, invisible to outcome-only evaluation, directly measurable.

modelDemoSignals.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
modelDemoSignals.description: Autonomous robots that interact with people must make safe and efficient decisions under human-induced uncertainty, such as their preferences, goals, competency, and willingness to cooperate. Safety filters are a popular approach for ensuring safety in interactive robotics, since their modular design separates safety from performance, allowing robots to operate safely around people with minimal impact on task efficiency. While traditional safety filters typically operate only in the physical spa
modelDemoSignals.summary: Autonomous robots that interact with people must make safe and efficient decisions under human-induced uncertainty, such as their preferences, goals, competency, and willingness to cooperate. Safety filters are a popular approach for ensuring safety in interactive robotics, since their modular design separates safety from performance, allowing robots to operate safely around people with minimal impact on task efficiency. While traditional safety filters typically operate only in the physical space, neglecting the robot's ability to learn and adapt online, the recently proposed belief-space safety filter (BeliefSF) reasons about robot safety in closed-loop with runtime inference that actively reduces the robot's uncertainty online, thereby reducing conservativeness in filtering. However, providing formal safety guarantees for robots deploying BeliefSF remains a significant challenge due to errors in runtime inference and neural approximation of safety filters required to handle the high dimensionality of belief spaces. In this paper, we propose an algorithmic approach to certify high-probability safety of BeliefSF using conformal prediction, while explicitly accounting for the reliability of the robot's runtime inference module. Our method leverages the structure of belief-space safety filtering by focusing verification on a region where inference is expected to be reliable. It preserves the simplicity and sample complexity of standard conformal prediction, yet can certify a substantially less conservative safety filter. Through a simulated human-vehicle interaction benchmark, we show that our approach verifies a significantly more permissive belief-space safety filter than a standard conformal prediction baseline.

aihotHighlights.title: 为了不花那120刀，我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill
aihotHighlights.summary: 作者受一条推文启发，使用Codex对自己的MacBook进行了只读存储分析，发现了B站缓存视频等大量可清理空间（激进方案超140G）。为替代收费软件CleanMyMac，作者制作并开源了一个跨平台（支持Mac/Windows）的AI清理skill。该skill会扫描文件并生成可交互的HTML报告，通过三色分级（绿灯可放心清理、黄灯需人工判断、红灯禁止动）直观展示，并提供安全执行按钮。实测清理后释放了近120G空间，相比CleanMyMac仅扫描出的15.8G，其信息更透明、建议更详细。

aihotHighlights.title: Anthropic 开发者分享 Claude Code「理解验证」工作流
aihotHighlights.summary: Anthropic 核心开发者分享了一套用于 Claude Code 的「理解验证」工作流。该工作流将 AI 定位为"高效且睿智的教师"，成功标准不仅是完成任务，更是确保人类对问题、方案及影响有可复述、可辩护的掌握。它通过增量教学、用户复述、清单+测验等方式，围绕问题域、方案域和语境域三条轴线展开，具体包含8个可执行步骤，强调在进入下一阶段前需确认用户已真正理解。此工作流旨在对抗长会话中人类易沦为"审批按钮"的"智能体黑箱"问题，强制沉淀决策上下文，实现可审计的深度理解。

aihotHighlights.title: Sam Altman强调AI发展应以人为本
aihotHighlights.summary: Sam Altman在采访中表示，AI不应被设计为追求脱离人类需求的目标，人类必须始终处于AI发展的中心。他批判了行业内"AI将摧毁大量工作"等言论，认为人们担忧的并非AI带来的好处，而是自身在未来的角色、经济前景与自主权。他指出，AI行业的失败在于未能清晰解释人类如何在每一步保持对未来的控制权，以及如何在AI时代继续拥有充实、有意义的生活。

items.title: Anthropic 保密向 SEC 提交 S-1 草案
items.summary: Anthropic， PBC 今日保密向美国证券交易委员会提交了 S-1 表格草案，计划进行普通股的首次公开发行。这使其在 SEC 完成审核后拥有上市的选择权。IPO 的具体发行股数和价格尚未确定，将取决于市场条件等因素。公司近期刚完成由 Altimeter Capital 等领投的 650 亿美元 H 轮融资，估值达 9650 亿美元，并发布了 Claude Opus 4.8 模型。

items.title: SK会长崔泰源：SK海力士计划未来五年内晶圆产能翻倍
items.description: SK海力士会长崔泰源宣布，计划在未来五年内将整体晶圆产能提高一倍，以应对AI普及带来的持续存储供应短缺。他预测AI数据中心和AI PC的普及将持续拉动存储需求，供需紧张局面可能延续至2030年。SK海力士将投入大规模资金用于设备、建设等扩张，尽管面临前置时间长（新建晶圆厂至少三年）和资源成本上涨等挑战。目前，SK海力士市值已首次突破1万亿美元。

items.title: 为了不花那120刀，我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill
items.description: 作者受一条推文启发，使用Codex对自己的MacBook进行了只读存储分析，发现了B站缓存视频等大量可清理空间（激进方案超140G）。为替代收费软件CleanMyMac，作者制作并开源了一个跨平台（支持Mac/Windows）的AI清理skill。该skill会扫描文件并生成可交互的HTML报告，通过三色分级（绿灯可放心清理、黄灯需人工判断、红灯禁止动）直观展示，并提供安全执行按钮。实测清理后释放了近120G空间，相比CleanMyMac仅扫描出的15.8G，其信息更透明、建议更详细。

items.title: 阶跃星辰Step 3.7 Flash发布，专为高效推理设计
items.description: 阶跃星辰发布其推理优化型模型Step 3.7 Flash。该模型为196B MoE架构，从设计之初就专注于推理效率。其采用多矩阵分解注意力机制，使KV-cache成本仅为DeepSeek模型的约22%；同时通过注意力与FFN解耦技术，实现了硬件优化的高效服务。该模型已通过Fireworks AI提供，采用Apache 2.0许可，并可用于构建智能体应用。

items.title: NVIDIA JetPack 7.2支持内存优化的边缘智能体部署
items.description: NVIDIA JetPack 7.2发布，支持一键部署开源NVIDIA NemoClaw堆栈，该堆栈为OpenClaw添加了隐私与安全控制。同时引入NVIDIA agent skills for Jetson，为Jetson设备提供智能体技能。该版本优化了内存效率，旨在加速AI代理从数字世界向物理环境的边缘部署。

items.title: Anthropic 开发者分享 Claude Code「理解验证」工作流
items.description: Anthropic 核心开发者分享了一套用于 Claude Code 的「理解验证」工作流。该工作流将 AI 定位为"高效且睿智的教师"，成功标准不仅是完成任务，更是确保人类对问题、方案及影响有可复述、可辩护的掌握。它通过增量教学、用户复述、清单+测验等方式，围绕问题域、方案域和语境域三条轴线展开，具体包含8个可执行步骤，强调在进入下一阶段前需确认用户已真正理解。此工作流旨在对抗长会话中人类易沦为"审批按钮"的"智能体黑箱"问题，强制沉淀决策上下文，实现可审计的深度理解。

items.title: Sam Altman强调AI发展应以人为本
items.description: Sam Altman在采访中表示，AI不应被设计为追求脱离人类需求的目标，人类必须始终处于AI发展的中心。他批判了行业内"AI将摧毁大量工作"等言论，认为人们担忧的并非AI带来的好处，而是自身在未来的角色、经济前景与自主权。他指出，AI行业的失败在于未能清晰解释人类如何在每一步保持对未来的控制权，以及如何在AI时代继续拥有充实、有意义的生活。

items.title: 为了不花那120刀，我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill
items.description: 作者受一条推文启发，使用Codex对自己的MacBook进行了只读存储分析，发现了B站缓存视频等大量可清理空间（激进方案超140G）。为替代收费软件CleanMyMac，作者制作并开源了一个跨平台（支持Mac/Windows）的AI清理skill。该skill会扫描文件并生成可交互的HTML报告，通过三色分级（绿灯可放心清理、黄灯需人工判断、红灯禁止动）直观展示，并提供安全执行按钮。实测清理后释放了近120G空间，相比CleanMyMac仅扫描出的15.8G，其信息更透明、建议更详细。

items.title: 阶跃星辰Step 3.7 Flash发布，专为高效推理设计
items.description: 阶跃星辰发布其推理优化型模型Step 3.7 Flash。该模型为196B MoE架构，从设计之初就专注于推理效率。其采用多矩阵分解注意力机制，使KV-cache成本仅为DeepSeek模型的约22%；同时通过注意力与FFN解耦技术，实现了硬件优化的高效服务。该模型已通过Fireworks AI提供，采用Apache 2.0许可，并可用于构建智能体应用。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic 保密向 SEC 提交 S-1 草案
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic 保密向 sec 提交 s 1 草案
title:anthropic confidentially submits draft s 1 to the sec

items.title: Anthropic 保密向 SEC 提交 S-1 草案
items.summary: Anthropic， PBC 今日保密向美国证券交易委员会提交了 S-1 表格草案，计划进行普通股的首次公开发行。这使其在 SEC 完成审核后拥有上市的选择权。IPO 的具体发行股数和价格尚未确定，将取决于市场条件等因素。公司近期刚完成由 Altimeter Capital 等领投的 650 亿美元 H 轮融资，估值达 9650 亿美元，并发布了 Claude Opus 4.8 模型。

trendEntities.title: SK会长崔泰源：SK海力士计划未来五年内晶圆产能翻倍
normalizedKeys: title:sk会长崔泰源 sk海力士计划未来五年内晶圆产能翻倍

items.title: SK会长崔泰源：SK海力士计划未来五年内晶圆产能翻倍
items.summary: SK海力士会长崔泰源宣布，计划在未来五年内将整体晶圆产能提高一倍，以应对AI普及带来的持续存储供应短缺。他预测AI数据中心和AI PC的普及将持续拉动存储需求，供需紧张局面可能延续至2030年。SK海力士将投入大规模资金用于设备、建设等扩张，尽管面临前置时间长（新建晶圆厂至少三年）和资源成本上涨等挑战。目前，SK海力士市值已首次突破1万亿美元。

trendEntities.title: 为了不花那120刀，我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill
normalizedKeys: title:为了不花那120刀 我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill

items.title: 为了不花那120刀，我把电脑清理软件做成了开源skill
items.summary: 作者受一条推文启发，使用Codex对自己的MacBook进行了只读存储分析，发现了B站缓存视频等大量可清理空间（激进方案超140G）。为替代收费软件CleanMyMac，作者制作并开源了一个跨平台（支持Mac/Windows）的AI清理skill。该skill会扫描文件并生成可交互的HTML报告，通过三色分级（绿灯可放心清理、黄灯需人工判断、红灯禁止动）直观展示，并提供安全执行按钮。实测清理后释放了近120G空间，相比CleanMyMac仅扫描出的15.8G，其信息更透明、建议更详细。

items.summary: 阶跃星辰发布其推理优化型模型Step 3.7 Flash。该模型为196B MoE架构，从设计之初就专注于推理效率。其采用多矩阵分解注意力机制，使KV-cache成本仅为DeepSeek模型的约22%；同时通过注意力与FFN解耦技术，实现了硬件优化的高效服务。该模型已通过Fireworks AI提供，采用Apache 2.0许可，并可用于构建智能体应用。

items.title: NVIDIA JetPack 7.2支持内存优化的边缘智能体部署
items.summary: NVIDIA JetPack 7.2发布，支持一键部署开源NVIDIA NemoClaw堆栈，该堆栈为OpenClaw添加了隐私与安全控制。同时引入NVIDIA agent skills for Jetson，为Jetson设备提供智能体技能。该版本优化了内存效率，旨在加速AI代理从数字世界向物理环境的边缘部署。

trendEntities.title: Anthropic 开发者分享 Claude Code「理解验证」工作流
normalizedKeys: title:anthropic 开发者分享 claude code 理解验证 工作流

items.title: Anthropic 开发者分享 Claude Code「理解验证」工作流
items.summary: Anthropic 核心开发者分享了一套用于 Claude Code 的「理解验证」工作流。该工作流将 AI 定位为"高效且睿智的教师"，成功标准不仅是完成任务，更是确保人类对问题、方案及影响有可复述、可辩护的掌握。它通过增量教学、用户复述、清单+测验等方式，围绕问题域、方案域和语境域三条轴线展开，具体包含8个可执行步骤，强调在进入下一阶段前需确认用户已真正理解。此工作流旨在对抗长会话中人类易沦为"审批按钮"的"智能体黑箱"问题，强制沉淀决策上下文，实现可审计的深度理解。

trendEntities.title: Sam Altman强调AI发展应以人为本
normalizedKeys: title:sam altman强调ai发展应以人为本

items.title: Sam Altman强调AI发展应以人为本
items.summary: Sam Altman在采访中表示，AI不应被设计为追求脱离人类需求的目标，人类必须始终处于AI发展的中心。他批判了行业内"AI将摧毁大量工作"等言论，认为人们担忧的并非AI带来的好处，而是自身在未来的角色、经济前景与自主权。他指出，AI行业的失败在于未能清晰解释人类如何在每一步保持对未来的控制权，以及如何在AI时代继续拥有充实、有意义的生活。

items.title: Auto Router 新增成本质量权衡参数
items.summary: Auto Router 现在允许你调整其在成本与质量之间的权衡。 新增 `cost_quality_tradeoff` 参数，范围 0 到 10： 设为 0 时，它总是选择最强大的模型，无论价格如何。设为 10 时，最便宜的模型胜出。

trendEntities.title: 智谱：建议 A 股发行并在科创板上市
normalizedKeys: title:智谱 建议 a 股发行并在科创板上市

items.title: 智谱：建议 A 股发行并在科创板上市
items.summary: 智谱计划向中国监管机构申请发行A股并在科创板上市。发行股份数量占发行完成后总股本的2%至8%，预计全部为新股，原股东不发售。本次发行募集资金净额将投资于人工智能通用基座大模型、大模型MaaS一站式服务平台及补充流动资金。此外，公司拟将英文名称由"Knowledge Atlas Technology Joint Stock Company Limited"变更为"Z.AI Co.， Ltd."。

trendEntities.title: SenseNova新模型解决AI图表生成难题
normalizedKeys: title:sensenova新模型解决ai图表生成难题

items.title: SenseNova新模型解决AI图表生成难题
items.summary: 大多数AI模型在生成图表时存在数值错误（如负值显示为正）、柱状图位置偏移、元素关系混乱等问题。SenseNova-U1-8B-MoT-Infographic（SenseNova-U1）专为解决此类图表生成问题而设计，能够生成准确的图表，并支持实时调整设计和布局。项目在Hugging Face提供了模型，并在GitHub展示了效果案例。

items.title: Luma成立开放物理AI实验室攻克泛化难题
items.summary: 为改善人类生活，AI系统必须能帮助我们改善物理世界。阻碍我们迈向这一繁荣未来的是物理AI的泛化问题。 为解决此问题，我们在Luma建立了一个新的开放科学物理AI实验室。 阅读更多 → https：//lumalabs.ai/news/luma-open-physical-ai-lab

trendEntities.title: 超越LLM：为何可扩展的企业AI采用取决于智能体逻辑
normalizedKeys: title:超越llm 为何可扩展的企业ai采用取决于智能体逻辑

items.title: 超越LLM：为何可扩展的企业AI采用取决于智能体逻辑
items.summary: 可扩展的企业AI采用需超越大语言模型，依靠智能体逻辑来引导模型执行动态、长周期且受约束的企业工作流，从而提升质量、降低成本并建立信任。文中以IBM watsonx Code Assistant for Z为例，展示了智能体逻辑如何通过程序分析等技术，在理解大型遗留代码库时，相比纯LLM基线方法，能以约30倍更低的token消耗达到更优性能。在加速测试生成任务中，该方法亦能使代码覆盖度提升20%-45%，同时token消耗降低最高达15倍。

trendEntities.title: 作者分享使用 Codex App 开发的开源项目合集
normalizedKeys: title:作者分享使用 codex app 开发的开源项目合集

items.title: 作者分享使用 Codex App 开发的开源项目合集
items.summary: 作者分享了使用 Codex App 等工具开发的一系列开源项目，包括4个 Chrome 插件（如快捷提示词、新标签页）、4个网站（如艺术家风格对比、音乐展示）和5个 AI Skill（如论文解读、阅读助手）。项目基于 GPT-Image-2 API、Suno 等技术，并整合了 Read-frog、Hyperframe 等开源项目。

trendEntities.title: 开源与闭源模型在不同的增长曲线上
normalizedKeys: title:开源与闭源模型在不同的增长曲线上

items.title: 开源与闭源模型在不同的增长曲线上
items.summary: 当模型智能的微小提升能直接转化为实际价值时，开源与闭源模型正沿着不同的增长路径发展。闭源模型通过在特定场景下提供更高的边际智能来创造价值，而开源模型则在其他维度寻找增长点，两者形成了差异化的竞争格局。

trendEntities.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
normalizedKeys: title:mitigating perceptual judgment bias in multimodal llm as a judge via perceptual perturbation and reward modeling

items.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
items.description: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response
items.summary: Recent multimodal large language models have demonstrated strong reasoning ability, yet their reliability as automated evaluators remains limited by a critical weakness: when visual evidence conflicts with textual cues, MLLM judges tend to reward plausible narratives over perceptually correct answers. We identify and systematically analyze this phenomenon, which we term Perceptual Judgment Bias. Through controlled visual perturbations, existing multimodal judges frequently anchor on the response text instead of their own visual perception, leading to inconsistent and non-verifiable evaluations. To address this issue, we introduce the Perceptually Perturbed Judgment Dataset, which constructs minimally edited counterfactual responses that isolate perceptual errors and enable verifiable supervision. Building on this dataset, we develop a unified training framework that combines a structured GRPO-based reward with a batch-ranking objective, achieving coherent global ordering without explicit pairwise labels. Experiments across diverse MLLM-as-a-Judge benchmarks show that our approach substantially improves perceptual fidelity, ranking coherence, and alignment with human evaluation. Our results establish a scalable and generalizable pathway for training multimodal judges that are perceptually grounded, interpretable, and robust to visual-reasoning conflicts.
items.author: Seojeong Park, Jiho Choi, Junyong Kang, Seonho Lee, Jaeyo Shin, Hyunjung Shim
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, alignment, benchmark, evaluation

items.title: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
items.description: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong performance through instruction tuning, but real-world deployment requires them to continually acquire new vision-language capabilities, making Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) essential. To reduce inter-task interference and promote collaboration, recent methods often employ sparse architectures like Mixture of LoRA Experts with image-text similarity routing. However, tasks with distinct response structures could share highly
items.summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) achieve strong performance through instruction tuning, but real-world deployment requires them to continually acquire new vision-language capabilities, making Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) essential. To reduce inter-task interference and promote collaboration, recent methods often employ sparse architectures like Mixture of LoRA Experts with image-text similarity routing. However, tasks with distinct response structures could share highly similar visual-linguistic semantics and thus be wrongly routed to the same expert; image-text similarity alone is insufficient for reliable task assignment. For example, an expert in a grounding task requiring coordinate prediction may be biased toward producing short textual answers after learning semantically similar VQA tasks. This format-blind task assignment integrates heterogeneous response types into shared parameters, inducing gradient interference and ineffective expert collaboration. To address this problem, we propose ProtoAda, a prototype-guided adaptive tuning framework. ProtoAda introduces format-aware task prototypes to align task assignment and routing with both task semantics and output structure, and further consolidates format-compatible updates in a geometry-aware manner to effectively reuse and progressively refine existing parameters. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks demonstrate that ProtoAda achieves superior performance, especially on tasks whose answer structures are easily corrupted by sequential tuning.
items.author: Yu-Cheng Shi, Zhen-Hao Xie, Jun-Tao Tang, Da-Wei Zhou
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, benchmark

trendEntities.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
normalizedKeys: title:adacodec a predictive visual code for video mllms

items.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
items.description: Video is temporally redundant: adjacent frames usually share most objects, background, and layout. Yet existing video multimodal large language models (video MLLMs) usually encode each sampled frame as an independent RGB image, causing visual tokens to repeat content already present in earlier frames. This suggests a more direct video interface: send a full reference frame only when the scene cannot be predicted well from prior context, and otherwise transmit a compact description of inter-frame
items.summary: Video is temporally redundant: adjacent frames usually share most objects, background, and layout. Yet existing video multimodal large language models (video MLLMs) usually encode each sampled frame as an independent RGB image, causing visual tokens to repeat content already present in earlier frames. This suggests a more direct video interface: send a full reference frame only when the scene cannot be predicted well from prior context, and otherwise transmit a compact description of inter-frame changes. We call this interface a \emph{predictive visual code}, and instantiate it for video MLLMs as \textbf{AdaCodec}. AdaCodec spends full visual tokens on a reference frame only when its conditional predictive cost is high; otherwise, it encodes inter-frame changes, including motion and prediction residuals, as compact P-tokens. Across all eleven benchmarks, AdaCodec improves over the Qwen3-VL-8B per-frame RGB baseline at a matched visual-token budget. Even at $1/7$ the budget, AdaCodec with 32k tokens surpasses the 224k baseline on all long-video benchmarks; on five general-video benchmarks, it raises the average score while substantially cutting time-to-first-token from 9.26s to 1.62s.
items.author: Haowen Hou, Zhen Huang, Zheming Liang, Qingyi Si, Chenglin Li, Shuai Dong, Kele Shao, Ruilin Li, Dianyi Wang, Nan Duan, Jiaqi Wang
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, rag, benchmark

trendEntities.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
normalizedKeys: title:clinenv an interactive multi stage long horizon ehr environment for agents

items.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
items.description: Clinical practice is not the selection of an answer from enumerated options: a physician gathers heterogeneous information incrementally and commits to sequential, irreversible decisions under uncertainty. Static benchmarks cannot probe and existing interactive medical benchmarks each compromise on at least one of them. We present ClinEnv, an interactive benchmark that evaluates LLMs as attending physicians over real inpatient admissions under a paradigm we term Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation
items.summary: Clinical practice is not the selection of an answer from enumerated options: a physician gathers heterogeneous information incrementally and commits to sequential, irreversible decisions under uncertainty. Static benchmarks cannot probe and existing interactive medical benchmarks each compromise on at least one of them. We present ClinEnv, an interactive benchmark that evaluates LLMs as attending physicians over real inpatient admissions under a paradigm we term Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation. Each case is automatically constructed into an ordered sequence of decision stages; at every stage the model must actively query four specialized agents before committing to medications, procedures, and diagnoses. ClinEnv scores both what the model decides, through deterministic ontology-grounded matching, and how it gathers information. Across seven models, the strongest reaches only 0.31 decision F1, and outcome quality is sharply decoupled from process quality. Difficulty concentrates in management decisions and later stages, where models recover discharge diagnoses far more reliably than management actions (0.51 vs. 0.17 F1) and continue to issue redundant queries as cases progress. ClinEnv makes this information-acquisition gap, invisible to outcome-only evaluation, directly measurable.
items.author: Yuxing Lu, Yushuhong Lin, Wenqi Shi, J. Ben Tamo, Xukai Zhao, Jinzhuo Wang, May Dongmei Wang
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, agent, benchmark, evaluation

trendEntities.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
normalizedKeys: title:permissive safety through trusted inference verifiable belief space neural safety filters for assured interactive robotics

items.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
items.description: Autonomous robots that interact with people must make safe and efficient decisions under human-induced uncertainty, such as their preferences, goals, competency, and willingness to cooperate. Safety filters are a popular approach for ensuring safety in interactive robotics, since their modular design separates safety from performance, allowing robots to operate safely around people with minimal impact on task efficiency. While traditional safety filters typically operate only in the physical spa
items.summary: Autonomous robots that interact with people must make safe and efficient decisions under human-induced uncertainty, such as their preferences, goals, competency, and willingness to cooperate. Safety filters are a popular approach for ensuring safety in interactive robotics, since their modular design separates safety from performance, allowing robots to operate safely around people with minimal impact on task efficiency. While traditional safety filters typically operate only in the physical space, neglecting the robot's ability to learn and adapt online, the recently proposed belief-space safety filter (BeliefSF) reasons about robot safety in closed-loop with runtime inference that actively reduces the robot's uncertainty online, thereby reducing conservativeness in filtering. However, providing formal safety guarantees for robots deploying BeliefSF remains a significant challenge due to errors in runtime inference and neural approximation of safety filters required to handle the high dimensionality of belief spaces. In this paper, we propose an algorithmic approach to certify high-probability safety of BeliefSF using conformal prediction, while explicitly accounting for the reliability of the robot's runtime inference module. Our method leverages the structure of belief-space safety filtering by focusing verification on a region where inference is expected to be reliable. It preserves the simplicity and sample complexity of standard conformal prediction, yet can certify a substantially less conservative safety filter. Through a simulated human-vehicle interaction benchmark, we show that our approach verifies a significantly more permissive belief-space safety filter than a standard conformal prediction baseline.
items.author: Haimin Hu
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: rag, safety, benchmark

trendEntities.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
normalizedKeys: title:from layers to submodules rethinking granularity in replacement based llm compression

items.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
items.description: Post-training compression of Large Language Models (LLMs) removes entire architectural components, either deleting them or replacing them with fitted modules. Existing replacement-based methods share two design constraints: full-layer granularity and contiguous selection. We argue that this is overly restrictive: in fact, redundancy in pretrained transformers is not confined to contiguous regions, nor does it evenly distribute between Attention and FeedForward outputs, implying that different st
items.summary: Post-training compression of Large Language Models (LLMs) removes entire architectural components, either deleting them or replacing them with fitted modules. Existing replacement-based methods share two design constraints: full-layer granularity and contiguous selection. We argue that this is overly restrictive: in fact, redundancy in pretrained transformers is not confined to contiguous regions, nor does it evenly distribute between Attention and FeedForward outputs, implying that different strategies best approximate different submodule types and that removable components need not cluster within contiguous depth ranges. Based on this intuition, we introduce SubFit (Submodule-level Fitted residual replacement), which compresses LLMs at the submodule level: Attention and FeedForward submodules are selected non-contiguously, and each receives its own lightweight fitted residual bypass. SubFit operates post-training and requires only calibration data. Across ten LLMs (five base, five instruction-tuned), five sparsity levels from 12.5% to 37.5%, and four replacement-based baselines, SubFit achieves the best aggregate perplexity-accuracy trade-off across the evaluated sparsity levels, with larger gains under aggressive compression. At 25% sparsity, it retains 84.6% of dense downstream accuracy and incurs 2.42x perplexity degradation, against 81.6% and 4.34x for the strongest baselines, while delivering measurable inference speedup and KV-cache savings. Code is available at https://github.com/eliacunegatti/SubFit.

sourceItems.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
sourceItems.description: Post-training compression of Large Language Models (LLMs) removes entire architectural components, either deleting them or replacing them with fitted modules. Existing replacement-based methods share two design constraints: full-layer granularity and contiguous selection. We argue that this is overly restrictive: in fact, redundancy in pretrained transformers is not confined to contiguous regions, nor does it evenly distribute between Attention and FeedForward outputs, implying that different st
sourceItems.summary: Post-training compression of Large Language Models (LLMs) removes entire architectural components, either deleting them or replacing them with fitted modules. Existing replacement-based methods share two design constraints: full-layer granularity and contiguous selection. We argue that this is overly restrictive: in fact, redundancy in pretrained transformers is not confined to contiguous regions, nor does it evenly distribute between Attention and FeedForward outputs, implying that different strategies best approximate different submodule types and that removable components need not cluster within contiguous depth ranges. Based on this intuition, we introduce SubFit (Submodule-level Fitted residual replacement), which compresses LLMs at the submodule level: Attention and FeedForward submodules are selected non-contiguously, and each receives its own lightweight fitted residual bypass. SubFit operates post-training and requires only calibration data. Across ten LLMs (five base, five instruction-tuned), five sparsity levels from 12.5% to 37.5%, and four replacement-based baselines, SubFit achieves the best aggregate perplexity-accuracy trade-off across the evaluated sparsity levels, with larger gains under aggressive compression. At 25% sparsity, it retains 84.6% of dense downstream accuracy and incurs 2.42x perplexity degradation, against 81.6% and 4.34x for the strongest baselines, while delivering measurable inference speedup and KV-cache savings. Code is available at https://github.com/eliacunegatti/SubFit.
sourceItems.author: Elia Cunegatti, Marcus Vukojevic, Erik Nielsen, Giovanni Iacca

items.title: HERO'S JOURNEY: Testing Complex Rule Induction with Text Games
items.description: We introduce HERO'S JOURNEY, a benchmark for rule induction in goal-directed episodic tasks, where agents must infer hidden rules from demonstrations and act on them through multi-step execution. HERO'S JOURNEY covers eight tasks across attribute and procedural induction families, each with four structural rule forms, controllable lexical grounding, and identifiability conditions. Evaluating state-of-the-art LLMs, we find that models show evidence of rule induction, but the ability is limited an
items.summary: We introduce HERO'S JOURNEY, a benchmark for rule induction in goal-directed episodic tasks, where agents must infer hidden rules from demonstrations and act on them through multi-step execution. HERO'S JOURNEY covers eight tasks across attribute and procedural induction families, each with four structural rule forms, controllable lexical grounding, and identifiability conditions. Evaluating state-of-the-art LLMs, we find that models show evidence of rule induction, but the ability is limited and uneven across tasks. Meanwhile, process execution adds an execution bottleneck for models, whereas surface semantics has minimal effect. Induction-specific steering methods improve performance on attribute tasks but show no reliable gains on procedural tasks, suggesting the gap in procedural induction remains an open challenge.
items.author: Anshun Asher Zheng, Kanishka Misra, David I. Beaver, Junyi Jessy Li

items.title: SN-WER: Script-Normalized WER for Multi-Script Indic ASR Evaluation
items.description: Word Error Rate (WER) is the dominant metric for automatic speech recognition (ASR), but it can overestimate errors when references and hypotheses encode the same words in different scripts. This issue is common in multilingual settings where ASR models may emit romanized text. We propose Script-Normalized WER (SN-WER), a training-free, evaluation-only scoring method that transliterates both reference and hypothesis text into a language-specific canonical script before computing WER. We evaluate
items.summary: Word Error Rate (WER) is the dominant metric for automatic speech recognition (ASR), but it can overestimate errors when references and hypotheses encode the same words in different scripts. This issue is common in multilingual settings where ASR models may emit romanized text. We propose Script-Normalized WER (SN-WER), a training-free, evaluation-only scoring method that transliterates both reference and hypothesis text into a language-specific canonical script before computing WER. We evaluate SN-WER on 5 Indic languages, 2 datasets, and 3 ASR models. On curated FLEURS data, SN-WER reduces inflated model gaps by up to 12%, while on noisier Common Voice data the reductions are smaller or inconsistent, indicating genuine recognition weaknesses rather than only script mismatch. Controlled stress tests show a 67% attenuation of artificial romanization-induced WER inflation, while lexical-substitution controls show near-identical sensitivity to semantic errors, with Delta SN-WER / Delta WER approximately 1.09. SN-WER is robust to transliterator choice, normalization changes, and shows low token-collision rates below 0.1% in the evaluated Indic setting. We argue that SN-WER should be reported alongside WER and CER as a companion metric for script-insensitive ASR evaluation, especially when transcripts feed downstream search, indexing, or multilingual LLM pipelines.
items.author: Priyaranjan Pattnayak
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: llm, evaluation

trendEntities.title: Transferable Self-Harm Surveillance from Emergency Department Triage Notes Using an Evidence-Augmented Machine Learning Approach
normalizedKeys: title:transferable self harm surveillance from emergency department triage notes using an evidence augmented machine learning approach

items.title: Transferable Self-Harm Surveillance from Emergency Department Triage Notes Using an Evidence-Augmented Machine Learning Approach
items.description: Self-harm is a major public health concern, but current surveillance relying on hospital presentations is inadequate due to the low sensitivity of diagnostic codes. Emergency Department (ED) triage notes, recorded at the initial point of contact, provide a succinct summary of presentations and an opportunity to identify self-harm. We developed a three-stage approach, augmenting traditional machine learning with large language model-based screening and evidence extraction to detect self-harm in E
items.summary: Self-harm is a major public health concern, but current surveillance relying on hospital presentations is inadequate due to the low sensitivity of diagnostic codes. Emergency Department (ED) triage notes, recorded at the initial point of contact, provide a succinct summary of presentations and an opportunity to identify self-harm. We developed a three-stage approach, augmenting traditional machine learning with large language model-based screening and evidence extraction to detect self-harm in ED triage notes. We assessed model transferability across three Australian hospitals. Our approach showed AUPRCs of 0.887 +/- 0.016 and 0.884 +/- 0.012 during internal and external validation. Prospectively, it achieved AUPRC of 0.881 +/- 0.008 at the development site, and 0.879 +/- 0.012 and 0.816 +/- 0.015 at two external sites without site-specific retraining. A key advantage of the approach is that it enables identification of the primary self-harm method with an accuracy of 95%, supporting more granular surveillance beyond binary classification.
items.author: Liuliu Chen, Gowri Rajaram, Eleanor Bailey, Katrina Witt, Michelle Lamblin, Jo Robinson, Mike Conway, Vlada Rozova

trendEntities.title: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
normalizedKeys: title:simsd simple speculative decoding in diffusion language models

items.title: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
items.description: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) have recently emerged as a promising alternative to autoregressive (AR) LLMs, offering faster inference through parallel or blockwise decoding. However, their masked language modeling formulation remains incompatible with standard token-level speculative decoding, one of the most effective acceleration techniques for AR models. In AR decoding, the causal mask preserves temporally valid token-level contexts, enabling a target model to verify multiple drafte
items.summary: Diffusion large language models (dLLMs) have recently emerged as a promising alternative to autoregressive (AR) LLMs, offering faster inference through parallel or blockwise decoding. However, their masked language modeling formulation remains incompatible with standard token-level speculative decoding, one of the most effective acceleration techniques for AR models. In AR decoding, the causal mask preserves temporally valid token-level contexts, enabling a target model to verify multiple drafted tokens in a single forward pass. In contrast, dLLMs rely on mask tokens and bidirectional attention, causing the effective context to change across denoising steps and preventing direct token-level speculative verification. To bridge this gap, we propose a simple but effective speculative decoding algorithm for diffusion language models, named SimSD, which mainly adopts a plug-and-play masking strategy that equips dLLMs with temporally valid token-level contexts for speculative decoding. Our method explicitly introduces reference tokens from draft-model predictions and designs an attention mask that regulates their interaction with current-step tokens, allowing dLLMs to compute valid logits for drafted tokens in a single forward pass. This restores the key verification ability provided by causal masking in AR models while preserving the parallel decoding advantages of dLLMs. The proposed method is training-free and can be flexibly integrated with other acceleration techniques such as KV cache and blockwise decoding. Experiments on SDAR-family dLLMs across four benchmarks show that our method achieves up to 7.46x higher decoding throughput while maintaining and even improving average generation quality.
items.author: Junxia Cui, Haotian Ye, Runchu Tian, Hongcan Guo, Jinya Jiang, Haoru Li, Chaojie Ren, Yiming Huang, Kaijie Zhu, Zhongkai Yu, Kun Zhou, Jingbo Shang

trendEntities.title: SkillHarm: Lifecycle-Aware Skill-Based Attacks via Automated Construction
normalizedKeys: title:skillharm lifecycle aware skill based attacks via automated construction

items.title: SkillHarm: Lifecycle-Aware Skill-Based Attacks via Automated Construction
items.description: Agent skills occupy a privileged position in the agent workflow, as agents are expected to implicitly follow and execute them, rendering third-party skills a vulnerable attack surface. Existing studies have revealed unsafe agent behaviors induced by skill-based attacks, but they primarily evaluate poisoned skills within a single task execution and enumerate harms through ad-hoc risk lists. To bridge these gaps, we introduce SkillHarm, a benchmark of skill-based attacks across the skill-use lifec
items.summary: Agent skills occupy a privileged position in the agent workflow, as agents are expected to implicitly follow and execute them, rendering third-party skills a vulnerable attack surface. Existing studies have revealed unsafe agent behaviors induced by skill-based attacks, but they primarily evaluate poisoned skills within a single task execution and enumerate harms through ad-hoc risk lists. To bridge these gaps, we introduce SkillHarm, a benchmark of skill-based attacks across the skill-use lifecycle, paired with a systematic taxonomy of skill-relevant risks. SkillHarm evaluates two attack scenarios: Fixed-Payload Poisoning (FPP), where a fixed poisoned skill package directly compromises any task session that invokes it, and Self-Mutating Poisoning (SMP), where an initially benign execution silently mutates persistent skill content, deferring harm until a subsequent reuse. It further defines 12 risk types based on the agent workflow component targeted by the harm: data pipelines, system environments, and agent autonomy. To instantiate these attacks at scale, we build AutoSkillHarm, an automated construction pipeline with coding agents driven by natural-language harnesses. The resulting benchmark contains 879 attack samples across 71 skills. Experiments show that current agents remain vulnerable with attack success rates up to 86.3% in FPP and 69.3% in SMP. Our analysis further reveals a latent risk: many apparent attack failures stem from the agent failing to engage with the poisoned file rather than genuine resistance, and current defenses still fail to reliably mitigate the threat.
items.author: Yuting Ning, Zhehao Zhang, Yash Kumar Lal, Boyu Gou, Junyi Li, Weitong Ruan, Chentao Ye, Rahul Gupta, Diyi Yang, Yu Su, Huan Sun
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, coding agent, benchmark, workflow

trendEntities.title: Tracking the Behavioral Trajectories of Adapting Agents
normalizedKeys: title:tracking the behavioral trajectories of adapting agents

items.title: Tracking the Behavioral Trajectories of Adapting Agents
items.description: Text files such as skill files, memory files, and behavioral configuration files play a central role in defining how modern agents act. Through edits by humans or the agents themselves, these files may evolve over time, directly steering the agent's behavior in future interactions. We present a methodology and framework for measuring agent $traits$ by defining traits as directions in the embedding space of a text embedding model. We train a linear model on labeled "before" versus "after" skill f
items.summary: Text files such as skill files, memory files, and behavioral configuration files play a central role in defining how modern agents act. Through edits by humans or the agents themselves, these files may evolve over time, directly steering the agent's behavior in future interactions. We present a methodology and framework for measuring agent $traits$ by defining traits as directions in the embedding space of a text embedding model. We train a linear model on labeled "before" versus "after" skill file diffs to learn a trait vector, then score arbitrary skill edits by projecting their embedding diffs onto this vector. Evaluated on 68 labeled skill diff pairs for the trait of propensity to seek sensitive data, our method achieves 91.2% sign classification accuracy and a Spearman rank correlation of $ρ= 0.82$ under leave-one-out cross-validation. We build this trait evaluation into a broader agent-to-agent protocol that enables one agent to evaluate another's skill file updates through a trusted intermediary.
items.author: Jonah Leshin, Manish Shah, Ian Timmis
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, embedding, evaluation

We present a methodology and framework for measuring agent $traits$ by defining traits as directions in the embedding space of a text embedding model. We train a linear model on labeled "before" versus "after" skill file diffs to learn a trait vector, then score arbitrary skill edits by projecting their embedding diffs onto this vector. Evaluated on 68 labeled skill diff pairs for the trait of propensity to seek sensitive data, our method achieves 91.2% sign classification accuracy and a Spearman rank correlation of $ρ= 0.82$ under leave-one-out cross-validation. We build this trait evaluation into a broader agent-to-agent protocol that enables one agent to evaluate another's skill file updates through a trusted intermediary.
sourceItems.author: Jonah Leshin, Manish Shah, Ian Timmis
sourceItems.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: agent, embedding, evaluation

items.title: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment
items.description: Aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) with human values often degrades their general capabilities, termed the alignment tax. items.summary: Aligning Large Language Models (LLMs) with human values often degrades their general capabilities, termed the alignment tax. Existing methods mitigate this by balancing dual objectives, which heavily rely on massive general-purpose data or auxiliary reward models. In this paper, we argue that, because safety features are inherently sparse within the output distribution, alignment requires localized modifications rather than global trade-offs. To this end, we propose SafeSteer, which performs on-policy distillation confined to safety tokens. First, we construct a safety teacher via activation steering. Based on this teacher, we develop a safety token selection algorithm. Consequently, SafeSteer restricts the reverse KL penalty to these tokens during training to preserve general capabilities. Experimental results across diverse models show that our SafeSteer achieves a superior trade-off between safety and general capability compared with existing methods, attaining strong safety performance on seven safety benchmarks with only minimal degradation on five general capability benchmarks. Notably, SafeSteer requires only 100 harmful samples without using any general-purpose data, less than 1% of what previous baselines used, considerably reducing alignment cost. More details are on our project page at https://anjingkun.github.io/SafeSteer.
items.author: Hao Li, Jingkun An, Zijun Song, Pengyu Zhu, Rui Li, Hao Wang, Wendi Feng, Yesheng Liu, Lijun Li, Jin-Ge Yao, Lei Sha
items.recommendedReason: Matched AI developer keywords: large language model, llm, alignment, safety, benchmark
